codeReview.summary: The fix closes the mocked REST-to-Realtime websocket-hop gap for VAL-CROSS-009, but the paired mock/live equivalence proof for VAL-CROSS-004 still synthesizes the live realtime event ordering instead of deriving it from observed live report data.

issues.description: The live-side normalization fabricates `event_ordering` from the surface name (`normalize_event_ordering(&surface)`) instead of reading any ordering evidence from the live report, while `tests/live_cross_surface_smoke.rs` only records `surface`, `status_class`, `request_id`, and `terminal_interpretation` for the paired output. Because the realtime live report never carries observed ordering data, the paired mock/live report can still claim `["rest.client_secrets.create", "ws.session.created", "ws.close"]` even if the live proof stops short of demonstrating that ordering, so VAL-CROSS-004 is not fully closed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `sdk-surface-worker` skill still does not explicitly call out that proof-only cross-surface fixes may need a tiny combined REST+websocket local harness even when no production runtime change is required.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/staticpayload/Mainframe/openai-rust/.factory/skills/sdk-surface-worker/SKILL.md:17-18,25-30 describe proof-only validation-gap work but do not mention combined REST+websocket local harnesses; /Users/staticpayload/.factory/missions/beaa247f-5975-4da4-acc2-19deef388841/handoffs/2026-04-06T11-20-34-951Z__publish-ready-fix-cross-surface-equivalence-and-realtime-bootstrap__8e117048-9a92-4442-b5ba-6382b9f98a98.json:75-76 records the same missing guidance in `suggestedChanges`.

summary: Fail: the fix successfully adds a real mocked websocket bootstrap hop for VAL-CROSS-009, but VAL-CROSS-004 remains incomplete because the paired live report hardcodes realtime event ordering instead of proving it from observed live report data.
